streams.name: Science (Group A PCM / Group B PCB)

departments.name: Agricultural Engineering
departments.eligibility: 10+2 with Physics + Mathematics. Biology or Chemistry as 3rd. ICAR AIEEA/State Ag CET. Min 55–60%.
departments.boardRequirement: Physics compulsory; Mathematics compulsory; Biology/Chemistry preferred as 3rd. Agriculture elective is excellent asset. ICAR AIEEA/State Ag CET.

departments.name: B.Sc Agriculture
departments.eligibility: 10+2 with Physics + Chemistry + Biology compulsory. Agriculture as elective is strong asset. ICAR AIEEA/State Ag CET. Min 50–60% PCB. Vocational Agriculture widely accepted at State Ag universities.
departments.boardRequirement: Physics: compulsory. Chemistry: compulsory. Biology: COMPULSORY. Agriculture (elective or vocational): excellent asset. PCB stream standard. CBSE: PCB with Agriculture elective is excellent. NIOS: 314+313+312. Vocational Agriculture widely accepted at State Ag universities. PCM without Biology: some Ag universities accept; most prefer Biology.
roles: PMFBY (Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana) Officer
Programme Officer — Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana
Farmer Trainer — System of Rice Intensification (SRI)
Cold Chain & Post-Harvest Loss Specialist — NGO
Participatory Guarantee System (PGS) Organic Certification Facilitator
USOF (Universal Service Obligation Fund) Project Coordinator

departments.name: B.Sc Biotechnology
departments.eligibility: 10+2 with Biology + Chemistry compulsory. Physics required by most. PCB is natural stream. PCM+B ideal. Min 60%.
departments.boardRequirement: Biology: COMPULSORY. Chemistry: COMPULSORY. Physics: required by most. Mathematics: useful but not always compulsory. PCB stream is standard. PCM without Biology rarely accepted. CBSE: PCB or PCMB. IB: Biology HL + Chemistry HL + Physics. Cambridge: Bio + Chem + Physics. NIOS: 314 + 313 + 312.

departments.name: B.Sc Food Science & Technology
departments.eligibility: 10+2 PCB or PCM. Chemistry + Biology strongly preferred. Home Science is an asset. Min 50–60%.
departments.boardRequirement: Chemistry: COMPULSORY. Biology: strongly preferred. Physics: required by many. Home Science (where available): excellent asset. CBSE: PCB with Chemistry. IB: Chemistry + Biology. Cambridge: Chemistry + Biology.

departments.name: B.Sc Geology & Earth Science
departments.eligibility: 10+2 with Physics + Chemistry compulsory. Maths or Biology as 3rd. Geography elective is strong asset. B.Sc Geology. Min 55–60%.
departments.boardRequirement: Physics: compulsory. Chemistry: compulsory. Maths: preferred; Biology also accepted. Geography/Env Science: strong asset. CBSE: PCM with Geography/Env Sci elective is excellent. IB: Geography HL is excellent. Cambridge: Geography is excellent asset.

departments.name: BBA / BMS
departments.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream. Min 50–60%. DU CUET, SET, private university entrances. All streams eligible.
departments.boardRequirement: No strict subject requirement. English: important. Mathematics: asset for quantitative methods. DU BMS: CUET-based. NMIMS/Symbiosis/Christ: own entrances testing English, Quant Aptitude, GK. All streams genuinely eligible.

departments.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream. Humanities preferred. Min 55–85% depending on college. CUET for DU.

departments.name: Fisheries Science
departments.eligibility: 10+2 PCB with Biology + Chemistry + Physics. Min 50–60%. State/Central Ag/Vet university entrance.
departments.boardRequirement: Biology: COMPULSORY. Chemistry: COMPULSORY. Physics: required. Same as BSc Agriculture eligibility. Agriculture/Environmental Science as elective: asset.

departments.name: Food Technology (Ag)
departments.eligibility: 10+2 PCB with Biology + Chemistry + Physics. Min 50–60%. State/Central Ag/Vet university entrance.
departments.boardRequirement: Biology: COMPULSORY. Chemistry: COMPULSORY. Physics: required. Same as BSc Agriculture eligibility. Agriculture/Environmental Science as elective: asset.

departments.name: Forestry
departments.eligibility: 10+2 PCB with Biology + Chemistry + Physics. Min 50–60%. State/Central Ag/Vet university entrance.
departments.boardRequirement: Biology: COMPULSORY. Chemistry: COMPULSORY. Physics: required. Same as BSc Agriculture eligibility. Agriculture/Environmental Science as elective: asset.

departments.name: Geography
departments.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream. Humanities preferred. Min 55–85% depending on college. CUET for DU.
departments.boardRequirement: Geography in 10+2: STRONGLY PREFERRED. Environmental Science: asset. Statistics/Mathematics: for quantitative geography. CBSE/State: Geography in all streams. IB: Geography HL direct prep. Cambridge: Geography A-Level direct prep.

departments.name: Film & Television Production

Industrial Robot Programmer (ABB / FANUC / KUKA / Yaskawa)

departments.name: Architecture (B.Arch)
departments.eligibility: 10+2 with Mathematics compulsory. Physics usually required. NATA mandatory. JEE Paper 2 for NITs. Min 50% PCM.
departments.boardRequirement: Mathematics: COMPULSORY. Physics: required by most. NATA: mandatory for COA-affiliated colleges – tests Drawing Aptitude, Mathematics, Physics/Chemistry knowledge. Fine Arts/Drawing: strong asset for NATA drawing component. Without Maths: not eligible anywhere.
roles: Building Energy Simulation Engineer (EnergyPlus / eQUEST / DesignBuilder)

departments.name: Fashion Design
departments.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream. Portfolio/aptitude test or English proficiency is primary criterion. Min 50% aggregate. Wide eligibility.
departments.boardRequirement: NIFT Entrance: Creative Ability Test (CAT) + General Ability Test (GAT). NID: Design Aptitude Test. Fine Arts/Drawing: strong asset. Home Science: relevant (textiles). No mandatory academic subject.

departments.name: Game Design & Development
departments.eligibility: 10+2 with Mathematics compulsory. Computer Science/IP preferred. Min 60%. Private institutes primarily.
departments.boardRequirement: Mathematics: COMPULSORY. Computer Science or IP: strongly preferred. Same as BCA/BSc CS eligibility in most cases. Programming background (even self-taught) is a strong differentiator.
roles: Professional eSports Player — BGMI (Battlegrounds Mobile India)

departments.name: Sociology
departments.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream. Humanities preferred. Min 55–85% depending on college. CUET for DU.
departments.boardRequirement: Sociology in 10+2: direct prep. Psychology: supporting. Political Science: social policy. No mandatory subject.
roles: Urban Local Body Capacity Building Specialist

departments.name: Cognitive Science
departments.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream. Min 60%. Entrance test + essay/interview. All streams welcome.
departments.boardRequirement: No mandatory subject. Interdisciplinary curiosity is key. Relevant: Mathematics (for cognitive/env science), Social Sciences (for international/digital humanities), Sciences (for env science). Interview/essay matters.

departments.name: Philosophy
departments.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream. Humanities preferred. Min 55–85% depending on college. CUET for DU.
departments.boardRequirement: Philosophy in 10+2 (where available): direct prep. Logic (some boards): excellent. English: important for argumentation.
roles: NADA (National Anti-Doping Agency) Doping Control Officer

departments.name: BCA – Computer Applications
departments.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream. Mathematics strongly preferred – many colleges mandate it. Min 50–60%. No JEE needed.
departments.boardRequirement: Mathematics: most colleges require Maths in 10+2 for BCA. CS/IP: asset. DU BCA/BSc CS requires Maths. CBSE: PCM with CS/IP ideal; Commerce+Maths qualifies at many. Without Maths: limited options. IB: Maths AA/AI qualifies. Cambridge: Maths qualifies. NIOS: Maths (311) + CS (330) excellent.

departments.name: Information Technology
departments.eligibility: 10+2 with Physics + Mathematics. CS/IP as 3rd preferred. JEE/CET. Min 60–75%.
departments.boardRequirement: Physics compulsory; Mathematics compulsory; Computer Science or Informatics Practices as 3rd is strongly preferred. Same as CSE in most aspects.
roles: AWS Direct Connect / Azure ExpressRoute Specialist
USOF (Universal Service Obligation Fund) Project Coordinator
